title: Brainblast Verified Trap Instance (VTI)
description: The sellable atom of the Brainblast training-data platform: a machine-verified error→fix→test→proof record for one coding trap, pinned to a specific SDK version, with source provenance and corroboration. Each record is reward-gradable (RED on the vulnerable snippet, GREEN on the fixed snippet, both proven by Brainblast's own static checker / generated test). See ROADMAP-TRAINING-DATA.md, Stage 0.

schemaVersion.description: Schema contract version. Consumers should reject a major they do not understand; 1.1 is an additive superset of 1.0 (new redGreenProof.method values + optional corroboratingMethods).

trapId.description: Stable identifier for the trap class. Equals the Brainblast rule id that proves it (e.g. metaplex-nft-royalty-zero).

title.description: Human-readable one-line description of the trap, from the rule title.

sdk.description: The external component this trap lives in, pinned to a version range or version.

version.description: Version or semver range the trap applies to, e.g. >=0.18.0.

type.description: Component category, e.g. Blockchain, PaymentProcessor, Auth.

severity.description: Trap severity, inherited from the proving rule.

class.description: Trap taxonomy bucket — the failure family. Drives buyer filtering and the coverage heatmap (Stage 3).

vulnerable.description: The RED side: code that ships the trap.

path.description: Relative source path of the fixture this snippet came from.

detail.description: The checker's failDetail — why this is a trap.

fixed.description: The GREEN side: the corrected code.

path.description: Relative source path of the fixture this snippet came from.

detail.description: The checker's passDetail — why the fix resolves the trap.

generatedTest.description: The durable regression test that fails forever if the trap regresses. Null when the proving rule uses test.kind: none — in that case the static-checker RED/GREEN in redGreenProof is the complete proof (same treatment as Rust rules in scripts/prove.ts).

kind.description: Test template kind from the rule (e.g. vitest, none).

redGreenProof.description: The verification that makes this record reward-gradable. Produced by running Brainblast's own audit() over each fixture: the trap's rule must fail (RED) on the vulnerable snippet and pass (GREEN) on the fixed snippet.

red.description: True when the vulnerable fixture verified RED (trap present).

green.description: True when the fixed fixture verified GREEN (trap avoided).

method.description: How RED/GREEN was established — a single OracleMethod, or several '+'-joined when methods corroborate (e.g. 'static-checker+compiler'). Aligned with src/oracle/types.ts OracleMethod. The legacy 1.0 value 'generated-test' remains valid for back-compat.

checkKind.description: The rule's check.kind that produced the verdict.

corroboratingMethods.description: Additional oracle methods that independently confirmed RED→GREEN, beyond the primary in redGreenProof.method (mirrors proveWithBest's corroborations). Optional; added in schema 1.1.

provenance.description: Where the facts behind this trap came from — live-fetched source URLs and the pack that produced it.

sourceUrls.description: Official-doc URLs the trap's facts were sourced from.

pack.description: The Brainblast rule pack that produced this VTI.

exploit.description: Optional link to the real-world incident whose root cause this trap detects (from the rule's exploit block).

generator.description: Tool + version that emitted this record, e.g. gen-vti@0.1.0.

corroborationCount.description: Distinct repos/users that confirmed this trap's fix (from graduation telemetry). 0 for purely synthetic records with no field confirmation yet.

license.description: Licensing lot this record belongs to. synthetic-owned records are produced entirely from Brainblast's own packs/fixtures and carry no third-party consent obligation; contributor-grant-* records come from opt-in user contributions and MUST stay in a physically separate lot.

consentScope.description: What use the data may be put to. owned = Brainblast-owned, unrestricted. opt-in:* = scoped to the consent the contributor granted.
